data.title: HF Sinclair (DINO) Q3 EPS Rebound Tests Bearish Volatility Narrative
data.description:
HF Sinclair (DINO) has just posted its FY 2025 third quarter numbers, with revenue of US$7.3b and basic EPS of US$2.16, set against trailing 12 month earnings growth of 18.7%. The company has seen revenue move from US$7.2b in Q3 2024 to US$7.3b in Q3 2025, while quarterly EPS shifted from a loss of US$0.40 to EPS of US$2.16. The trailing net profit margin sits at 1.5% versus 1.1% last year, giving investors a clearer view of how the earnings profile is shaping up. Taken together, these results point to margins that are still relatively thin but moving in a direction that keeps profitability firmly in focus for shareholders.

See our full analysis for HF Sinclair.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the widely held narratives around HF Sinclair, highlighting where the numbers support the story and where they start to push back against it.

See what the community is saying about HF Sinclair

Advertisement

Profit swings show how cyclical refining can be

  • HF Sinclair moved from a net loss of US$213.3 million in Q4 2024 and US$3 million in Q1 2025 to net income of US$207.0 million in Q2 2025 and US$403.0 million in Q3 2025, highlighting how quickly profitability can shift within a few quarters.
  • Bears focus on that earlier loss making stretch and argue that refining earnings can remain volatile, yet the recent US$403.0 million profit tests the bearish view that pressure on margins will necessarily translate into persistently weak results.
    • The bearish narrative points to a first quarter net loss and pressure on refining EBITDA, which is consistent with the US$3 million loss in Q1 2025, but the swing to over US$200 million and then over US$400 million of profit suggests that segment performance can rebound when conditions are more favorable.
    • For a cautious investor, the pattern here is not one way traffic; it is a reminder that the same business model that produced losses in late 2024 and early 2025 is also capable of delivering solid profits when margins and volumes support it.

When you see profit move from losses to over US$400 million in a few quarters, it underlines how quickly the story can change in this kind of business, which is exactly what critics of the sector worry about.

🐻 HF Sinclair Bear Case

TTM earnings growth and 1.5% margin back the bullish case

  • Over the last twelve months HF Sinclair earned US$391.0 million of net income on US$26.9b of revenue, giving a 1.5% net margin and 18.7% earnings growth compared with the prior year, which is well above the roughly 2% per year growth rate over the past five years.
  • Bulls argue that tighter supply and resilient fuel demand support stronger and more durable profitability, and the combination of a 1.5% trailing margin and 18.7% earnings growth gives some numerical backing to that optimism, even though margins are still thin.
    • Supporters highlight that earnings are flagged as high quality, so the US$391.0 million of trailing profit is not just an accounting quirk but reflects underlying operations, which fits the bullish story about improved utilization, efficiency projects, and renewables starting to contribute.
    • At the same time, the margin is still only 1.5% on US$26.9b of sales, so while that aligns with the bullish claim of better profitability than in the past few years, it also reminds you that small changes in refining spreads can have an outsized impact on the bottom line.

Putting those trailing numbers next to the bullish narrative helps you see why some investors think HF Sinclair is on a better footing, but also why even small margin shifts can matter a lot here.

🐂 HF Sinclair Bull Case

Valuation signals pull in different directions

  • HF Sinclair trades at US$51.57 per share with a trailing P/E of 24.3x, compared with 14.5x for the wider US Oil & Gas industry and 19x for peers, while a DCF fair value of US$204.63 points to a very large gap between that model and the current price.
  • What stands out is the tension between a premium P/E multiple and that high DCF fair value, which both bulls and bears use differently, and the numbers give you a way to test each side rather than taking either at face value.
    • Supportive of the bullish angle, the combination of 18.7% trailing earnings growth and a current price far below the US$204.63 DCF fair value is exactly the sort of setup bulls point to when they say the market may not be paying fully for HF Sinclair’s cash flow potential.
    • In line with the cautious view, the 24.3x trailing P/E versus 14.5x for the industry and 19x for peers suggests the market is already assigning HF Sinclair a richer multiple than many competitors, which bears see as leaving less room for error, especially with a dividend yield of 3.88% that is not strongly covered by earnings.

    After all this, are you feeling more bullish or cautious about HF Sinclair? If you want to move quickly and base your view on the underlying data, take a closer look at the balance of 2 key rewards and 1 important warning sign before you decide where you stand.

data.title: Quad Graphics (QUAD) Return To Profitability Tests Bullish Margin Expansion Narrative
data.description:
Quad/Graphics (QUAD) closed out FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of $630.6 million and basic EPS of $0.23, alongside net income of $11.1 million. Over the past few quarters, the company has seen revenue move from $708.4 million and EPS of $0.10 in Q4 2024 through $629.4 million and $0.12 in Q1 2025, $571.9 million and roughly breakeven EPS in Q2 2025, then $588 million and $0.21 in Q3 2025. This frames a year where profitability settled in at a trailing twelve month EPS of $0.57 on $2.4 billion of revenue. For investors, the latest print points to margins that are back in positive territory, with the key question now around how durable that profitability proves to be.

See our full analysis for Quad/Graphics.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the major narratives around Quad/Graphics, and where the story the numbers tell might challenge those views.

See what the community is saying about Quad/Graphics

Advertisement

TTM profit of $27 million after prior losses

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Quad/Graphics moved from a loss of $50.9 million at the end of Q4 2024 to net income of $27 million by Q4 2025. Over the same period, TTM EPS shifted from a loss of $1.07 per share to earnings of $0.57 per share.
  • What stands out against the bullish view that earnings quality is improving is the mix of factors behind this swing. The data cites both multi year earnings growth of 13.4% a year and a $21.8 million one off loss in the last twelve months, so investors need to separate:
    • The core pattern of quarterly net income turning positive in 2025, from $5.8 million in Q1 to $11.1 million in Q4, which supports the idea of a more profitable business model.
    • The impact of that one off $21.8 million charge in the TTM figures, which affects how clean the earnings trend really is when you apply the bullish margin expansion story.

Bulls argue that this move into positive TTM earnings could be the foundation for a stronger long term profit story, and you can see how that thinking lines up with the detailed narrative here: 🐂 Quad/Graphics Bull Case

P/E of 12.5x versus higher industry multiples

  • The company is trading on a trailing P/E of 12.5x, compared with 25.1x for the US Commercial Services industry and 31.6x for the peer average. A DCF fair value of US$16.33 sits well above the current share price of US$6.63.
  • Analysts' consensus narrative sees Quad/Graphics using AI driven automation and integrated marketing to grow higher margin services, and the current numbers partly support that value angle yet still leave questions, because:
    • The apparent gap between the share price of US$6.63, the DCF fair value of US$16.33 and the analyst target of US$9.10 points to the market pricing the stock below both modelled cash flow value and the consensus price target.
    • At the same time, TTM revenue of about US$2.4b at Q4 2025 compares with US$2.7b at Q3 2024, so the business is working off a lower sales base while trying to shift mix toward data driven marketing solutions.

Interest coverage and debt temper the bull case

  • One of the clearest risks in the data is that interest payments are not well covered by earnings, alongside a reported US$193 million of debt maturing in 2029 at a blended interest rate of 7.2% and an unstable dividend track record.
  • Bears focus on that financing pressure plus ongoing print headwinds, and the reported figures give some grounding to that cautious stance because:
    • Even with TTM net income of US$27 million by Q4 2025, the combination of interest costs and a past one off loss of US$21.8 million means cash flow has less room to absorb any setback in revenue or margins.
    • Recent revenue prints, from US$708.4 million in Q4 2024 to US$630.6 million in Q4 2025, show the business working through a smaller top line while still carrying that interest burden and credit investors' attention on leverage.

data.title: EVA Live launches client-facing NeuroServer web app to support enterprise growth
data.description:
Ad-tech company EVA Live (Nasdaq:GOAI) has launched its first client-facing web application for NeuroServer, its proprietary AI-powered ad server, marking what management describes as the transition into full commercial execution.

While framed as a product launch, the development carries broader implications for investors. The new web interface turns NeuroServer from a backend AI engine into a full enterprise platform, giving advertisers a centralized environment to launch, manage, and optimize campaigns in real time.

The company says early adoption has already begun following the beta release of NeuroServer two months ago, and as stated in yesterday’s press release, management expects the web application to become a “key growth driver and primary revenue engine throughout 2026 and beyond.”

Advertisement

Why the web application matters more than it sounds

NeuroServer itself has been in development for several years. The introduction of a structured, client-facing web application changes the commercial dynamic.

Instead of relying solely on direct integrations or managed-service workflows, EVA Live now offers advertisers a scalable interface that centralizes onboarding, campaign management, performance monitoring, and optimization.

From an investor’s perspective, this matters for three reasons:

  1. Scalability : A standardized web interface reduces friction in onboarding new clients and makes it easier to scale account volume without expanding headcount proportionally.
  2. Operating leverage : Automation reduces manual optimization work, potentially supporting margin expansion as revenue grows.
  3. Recurring revenue potential : A client-facing enterprise platform increases switching costs and creates the foundation for recurring revenue relationships.

The company said its real-time AI engine continuously analyzes performance data to optimize campaigns, mitigate fraud, and adjust targeting dynamically. Early client results indicate the system can increase ROI by up to 40%, according to management, though outcomes are likely to vary across customers.

The exact percentage is less important than the direction of travel. If clients see measurable performance improvements, retention and budget expansion typically follow.

Built for enterprise scale

Robert Vacaro, lead engineer on the NeuroServer web application, said the infrastructure was designed to support large advertisers managing significant campaign volumes.

“Our infrastructure is designed specifically for large-scale advertisers, enabling us to grow rapidly while delivering consistent performance and measurable results,” Vacaro said.

For investors, this signals an ambition beyond niche optimization tools. EVA Live is positioning NeuroServer as enterprise-grade infrastructure capable of handling significant advertising volumes.

The company said it is currently onboarding new online advertisers daily, suggesting early traction following the beta release.

Connecting product maturity to financial discipline

The launch also helps contextualize EVA Live’s recent capital allocation decisions, including the withdrawal of a planned equity raise.

A company still in heavy development mode often requires external funding to support product buildout. A company entering full commercial execution can begin focusing on scaling revenue from existing infrastructure.

“With the NeuroServer now live, we are shifting from development into full commercialization,” management said in a recent update.

If the platform scales as intended, revenue growth may increasingly depend on client acquisition and retention rather than product R&D cycles.

What investors should watch next

The launch itself is a milestone, but execution will matter more than messaging.

Investors will likely look for:

  1. Evidence of new client growth
  2. Data around recurring revenue contribution
  3. Indications for margins as campaign volume scales
  4. Customer retention and budget expansion metrics

If NeuroServer’s web application succeeds in turning performance gains into long-term enterprise relationships, EVA Live’s growth profile could shift toward a more predictable, software-like model.

About EVA Live

EVA Live is an AI-driven digital advertising company focused on delivering scalable performance marketing infrastructure. Following its Nasdaq uplisting in January 2026, the company has positioned NeuroServer as its core revenue platform for enterprise advertisers.

data.title: ALEC Holdings PJSC's (DFM:ALEC) Earnings May Just Be The Starting Point
data.description:
ALEC Holdings PJSC's (DFM:ALEC) strong earnings report was rewarded with a positive stock price move. We have done some analysis, and we found several positive factors beyond the profit numbers.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Zooming In On ALEC Holdings PJSC's Earnings

As finance nerds would already know, the accrual ratio from cashflow is a key measure for assessing how well a company's free cash flow (FCF) matches its profit. In plain english, this ratio subtracts FCF from net profit, and divides that number by the company's average operating assets over that period. The ratio shows us how much a company's profit exceeds its FCF.

Therefore, it's actually considered a good thing when a company has a negative accrual ratio, but a bad thing if its accrual ratio is positive. While it's not a problem to have a positive accrual ratio, indicating a certain level of non-cash profits, a high accrual ratio is arguably a bad thing, because it indicates paper profits are not matched by cash flow. To quote a 2014 paper by Lewellen and Resutek, "firms with higher accruals tend to be less profitable in the future".

Over the twelve months to December 2025, ALEC Holdings PJSC recorded an accrual ratio of -0.53. That implies it has very good cash conversion, and that its earnings in the last year actually significantly understate its free cash flow. Indeed, in the last twelve months it reported free cash flow of د.إ966m, well over the د.إ686.2m it reported in profit. ALEC Holdings PJSC shareholders are no doubt pleased that free cash flow improved over the last twelve months.

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

Our Take On ALEC Holdings PJSC's Profit Performance

As we discussed above, ALEC Holdings PJSC's accrual ratio indicates strong conversion of profit to free cash flow, which is a positive for the company. Based on this observation, we consider it possible that ALEC Holdings PJSC's statutory profit actually understates its earnings potential! Better yet, its EPS are growing strongly, which is nice to see. Of course, we've only just scratched the surface when it comes to analysing its earnings; one could also consider margins, forecast growth, and return on investment, among other factors. While it's really important to consider how well a company's statutory earnings represent its true earnings power, it's also worth taking a look at what analysts are forecasting for the future. Luckily, you can check out what analysts are forecasting by clicking here.

data.title: Bel Fuse (BELF.A) Quarterly Loss Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative
data.description:
Bel Fuse (BELF.A) has wrapped up FY 2025 with Q4 revenue of US$175.9 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.43, while trailing twelve month figures show revenue of US$675.5 million and EPS of US$4.87, underpinned by trailing earnings growth of 50.2% year over year. The company has seen revenue step up from US$534.8 million on a trailing basis in Q4 2024 to US$675.5 million in Q4 2025, with net income rising from US$41.0 million to US$61.5 million over the same period, setting the stage for investors to weigh improved margins and earnings trends as the key takeaway from this latest update.

See our full analysis for Bel Fuse.

With the numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this performance lines up against the main stories circulating around the stock, and where the data either backs or challenges those narratives.

See what the community is saying about Bel Fuse

Margins Hold Up At 9.1%

  • Bel Fuse is earning US$61.5 million of trailing net income on US$675.5 million of revenue, which lines up with the 9.1% net margin cited in the analysis and compares to 7.7% a year earlier.
  • Consensus narrative points to cost programs and mix helping profitability, and the numbers partly back that up:
    • Trailing earnings grew 50.2% year over year and five year annual earnings growth is 19.4%, which is consistent with a business that has been able to support higher margins over time.
    • At the same time, the consensus view highlights margin volatility in the Power segment and tariff related pressures, and the Q4 FY 2025 loss of US$5.4 million shows margins are not yet smooth across every period.

Earnings Growth Outruns Revenue

  • Trailing revenue sits at US$675.5 million while trailing net income is US$61.5 million, and over the last year earnings grew 50.2% compared with forecast revenue growth of 6.6% per year highlighted in the analysis.
  • Bulls focus on earnings power more than top line growth, and the data gives them some backing and some pushback:
    • The 19.4% annual earnings growth over five years and the 18.4% forecast earnings growth rate both indicate that profit has been growing faster than the revenue line cited at 6.6% growth.
    • However, Q4 FY 2025 shows a loss of US$5.4 million compared with profits in the prior three quarters, which suggests earnings can still swing even as the longer term averages look strong.

data.title: Tariff Cuts And US Investments Reframe TSMC Growth And Valuation Story
data.description:
• The US and Taiwan have agreed to lower tariffs on Taiwanese imports, directly affecting Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing’s export terms into the US market.
  • The Taiwanese government has pledged new investments in US semiconductor, energy, and AI sectors, with major commitments from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing.
  • These moves mark a shift in cross border cooperation that could influence how semiconductor supply chains and technologies are structured globally.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, NYSE:TSM, comes into this trade and investment reset with a recent share price of $362.26 and a 1 year return of 82.2%. Over 3 years, its return is about 4.3x, and over 5 years it stands at 192.4%. That backdrop helps explain why changes to tariff treatment and overseas commitments attract close attention from investors.

The lower US tariffs and fresh US focused investment plans could influence how Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing positions its production, partnerships, and capital spending over time. For you as an investor, the key questions now center on how these policy shifts might affect the company’s access to US demand, its cost structure, and its role in future semiconductor and AI build outs.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing.

We've flagged 1 risk for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing. See which could impact your investment.

The tariff cut from 20% to 15% directly reduces the cost of TSMC’s chips entering the US, which could improve its price competitiveness versus foundry peers such as Samsung Electronics and Intel. Combined with Taiwan’s pledge to invest more than US$250b in US semiconductor, energy, and AI production, including TSMC’s US$100b commitment, this agreement ties the company more closely to US AI and high performance computing build outs. For a contract foundry that relies on large, long term customer relationships, closer alignment with US policy and capital spending could support order visibility, especially as AI compute demand remains a central theme.

For you, the trade off to think about is that bigger US exposure may also come with higher fixed costs, execution complexity across multiple geographies, and potential political expectations around future capacity allocation. The agreement could influence where TSMC places its most advanced nodes versus mature-node production, how it prices wafers for different customers, and how its multi year capex plans balance between Taiwan and overseas fabs. Execution on the large US projects, supply chain reliability, and any shifts in customer mix across AI and non AI demand are likely to be key focus points after this announcement.

Advertisement

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Large US-focused capex programs can strain execution and may pressure returns if construction or ramp timelines slip.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that TSMC has a high level of non cash earnings, which can make reported profit quality harder to interpret.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow 17.47% per year, suggesting analysts see room for further profit expansion alongside these trade and investment moves.
  • 🎁 TSMC’s P/E of 28.9x is below the 42.3x semiconductor industry average and the company is described as trading at good value compared to peers and industry, which some investors may view as supportive if the new agreement helps sustain demand.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch how TSMC phases its US$100b US investment, including the timing of fab build outs, advanced-node roadmaps, and any updates on cost-sharing or incentives from US authorities. Keep an eye on US versus non US revenue mix, margins from overseas fabs compared with Taiwan, and whether AI-related partnerships translate into long term, contract-backed capacity commitments. Any further policy shifts between the US, Taiwan, and other chipmaking regions, as well as capex plans from large customers such as US hyperscalers, will also matter for how this agreement ultimately feeds into TSMC’s earnings profile and capital intensity.

Hackett Group FY 2025 earnings snapshot

Hackett Group (HCKT) has wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of about US$75.8 million and basic EPS of US$0.21, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of US$305.6 million and EPS of US$0.47 that sit against a net profit margin of 4.2% for the period.   • Trailing 12 month net income dropped from US$29.6 million a year ago to US$12.9 million, with net margin moving from 9.7% to 4.2%, even though revenue stayed close to US$305 million over both periods.
  • Critics highlight that weaker profitability complicates the bullish view that Gen AI investments will lift margins, because:
    • Analysts currently expect profit margins to move from 5.5% to 16.4% over three years, yet the latest trailing data points the other way, at 4.2% versus 9.7% last year.
    • Management is still dealing with higher SG&A and restructuring costs, while legacy areas like Oracle Solutions are described as facing a 20%+ year on year revenue hit. This aligns more with the cautious narrative than with a clean margin expansion story.

    • Leverage is called out as elevated at the same time as net margin sits at 4.2%, so the business is running with thinner profit cushions while also supporting interest and principal obligations.
    • The dividend, at 3.15%, is specifically tagged as weakly covered by earnings, which aligns with the cautious view that current profitability leaves less room for error if growth or Gen AI monetization does not arrive as expected.

data.title: Clean Harbors Net Margin Compression Challenges Bullish High P E Narrative
data.description:
Advertisement

Clean Harbors FY 2025 Earnings Snapshot

Clean Harbors (CLH) has posted another solid set of FY 2025 numbers, with Q3 revenue of US$1.5b and basic EPS of US$2.22, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of US$6.0b and EPS of US$7.23. The company has seen quarterly revenue move between US$1.4b and US$1.6b over the past six reported periods, while basic EPS has ranged from US$1.09 to US$2.47, giving investors a clear view of how earnings track against the current revenue base. With a trailing net margin of 6.5%, slightly below the 7.2% level a year earlier, the latest results put profitability squarely in focus for anyone watching how efficiently those revenues are being converted into bottom line returns.

See our full analysis for Clean Harbors.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up against the widely held stories about Clean Harbors, highlighting where the numbers back up the narrative and where they raise fresh questions.

Margins Ease Back From 7.2% To 6.5%

  • The trailing net profit margin sits at 6.5%, compared with 7.2% a year earlier, on about US$6.0b of revenue and US$388.4 million of net income.
  • Analysts' consensus view expects profit margins to reach 8.6% in about three years, which contrasts with the recent move from 7.2% to 6.5% and puts extra weight on the idea that investments in facilities and technology will improve efficiency over time.
    • The consensus narrative points to network expansion and logistics optimisation as margin drivers, yet the trailing numbers currently show margins below last year.
    • Forecast earnings growth of about 11.6% per year is built on those higher future margins, so the 6.5% trailing margin is a key figure to watch against that story.

Premium 38x P/E Versus Peers

  • Clean Harbors trades on a trailing P/E of 38x, compared with a 36.5x peer average and 25.1x for the wider US Commercial Services industry, while the current share price of US$276.25 sits about 24.1% below a DCF fair value of roughly US$364.03.
  • One point often highlighted for a bullish view is that a 38x P/E is paired with earnings that have grown 17.4% per year over five years, and a DCF fair value of US$364.03 that is higher than both the current price and the single allowed analyst target of US$272.54.
    • Bulls often point to that 17.4% five year earnings growth and the DCF fair value gap as support for paying a premium multiple despite margins being 6.5% today.
    • At the same time, revenue is forecast to grow around 5.1% per year, which is slower than the referenced US market at 10.3%, so the premium 38x P/E and the DCF fair value gap need to be weighed against more moderate top line growth assumptions.
On these numbers, if you want to see how supporters of the bullish case connect growth, margins, and valuation, the detailed bull narrative can help put the 38x P/E and DCF fair value gap into context. 🐂 Clean Harbors Bull Case

Earnings Growth Forecasts Versus 5.1% Revenue Pace

  • Analysts are assuming revenue growth of about 5.1% per year and earnings growth of roughly 11.6% per year, compared with the past five year earnings growth rate of 17.4% and trailing twelve month net income of about US$388.4 million.
  • Skeptics focus on the idea that environmental and technological shifts could cap longer term growth, and the current 6.5% net margin and high debt level give them specific figures to point to when they question whether earnings can reach the projected US$605.1 million by around 2028.
    • The bearish view flags that revenue growth at 5.1% per year sits below the 10.3% figure cited for the broader US market, which could matter if environmental rules or new technologies reduce demand for some of Clean Harbors' core services.
    • With forecasts calling for profit margins to move from 6.5% to 8.6%, any higher compliance or capital costs from new waste treatment technologies or regulations would directly affect the path to those higher earnings numbers.
If you are weighing these cautious points against the growth forecasts, the bear case narrative lays out how environmental and regulatory shifts could pressure those 11.6% earnings growth expectations. 🐻 Clean Harbors Bear Case

data.title: Liberty Global (LBTY.A) Quarterly Loss Narrows Yet LTM US$1.9b Loss Reinforces Bearish Narratives
data.description:
Liberty Global (LBTY.A) has reported another tough set of numbers for FY 2025, with Q3 revenue of US$1,207.1 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.27, alongside trailing 12 month revenue of US$4.8 billion and a basic EPS loss of US$5.47. Over recent periods, the company has seen revenue move from US$1,069.5 million in Q3 2024 to US$1,207.1 million in Q3 2025, while EPS has shifted from a loss of US$4.0 in Q3 2024 to a loss of US$0.27 in Q3 2025. Overall, the latest figures keep the spotlight firmly on margin pressure and the challenge of turning heavy losses into a more sustainable earnings profile.

See our full analysis for Liberty Global.

With the headline results now released, the next step is to assess how these margins and loss trends compare with the widely followed narratives around Liberty Global's long term earnings power and risk profile.

See what the community is saying about Liberty Global

Advertisement

LTM loss of US$1.9b keeps profitability under pressure

  • Over the trailing 12 months to Q3 2025, Liberty Global reported total revenue of US$4.8b and a net loss, excluding extra items, of US$1.9b, compared with a profit of US$1.8b in the prior reported LTM period that included Q4 2024.
  • Bears highlight that losses have grown at about 50.4% per year over the past five years, and the latest LTM loss of US$1.9b aligns with that concern, even though quarterly net losses have ranged from US$90.7 million in Q3 2025 to US$2.8b in Q2 2025.
    • This pattern supports the bearish view that earnings quality is weak and that the business has not been consistently profitable lately, given that recent quarters include multiple large loss figures alongside only a few earlier profitable quarters in 2024.
    • At the same time, the return to a smaller net loss of US$90.7 million in Q3 2025, compared with US$2.8b in Q2 2025, shows that results can move around quarter to quarter, which matters when bears assume losses will remain very large every period.
Over the last year of swings between multibillion losses and smaller quarterly losses, skeptics argue the big LTM loss still outweighs any short term improvements. 🐻 Liberty Global Bear Case

Revenue near US$4.8b while forecasts point to a 2.5% yearly decline

  • Trailing 12 month revenue sits at US$4.8b, and analysts expect revenue to fall by about 2.5% per year over the next three years, despite quarterly revenue holding between US$1.1b and US$1.3b in the last few periods.
  • Consensus narrative expects that network upgrades and bundled offers can help support revenue, yet the forecast 2.5% yearly revenue decline and the move from US$1.06b in Q3 2024 to US$1.21b in Q3 2025 creates a mixed picture for that view.
    • On one hand, consensus points to bundled services and fixed mobile convergence helping stabilize the top line, which is consistent with revenue in recent quarters staying above US$1.0b and LTM revenue of US$4.8b.
    • On the other hand, the projected 2.5% yearly decline shows that analysts as a group are not assuming strong growth, which limits how much the consensus argument leans on revenue expansion to support future profitability.

DCF fair value of US$36.56 vs share price of US$13.04

  • The shares trade at US$13.04 compared with a DCF fair value of US$36.56 and a P/S ratio of 0.9x, which is below both the US telecom industry average of 1.2x and the peer average of 1.3x.
  • Bullish investors point to the wide gap between the US$13.04 share price and the US$36.56 DCF fair value, as well as the discount P/S multiple, but this optimism sits against forecasts that the company will remain unprofitable over the next three years.
    • The lower 0.9x P/S multiple compared with industry and peers matches the bullish idea that the stock is priced cheaply relative to revenue, especially given LTM revenue of US$4.8b.
    • However, the expectation that the company stays loss making for at least three years means the bullish argument rests more on future margin improvement than on current earnings, which are still negative at US$1.9b over the last 12 months.
If you want to see how optimistic investors connect this valuation gap to future margin recovery, take a look at the 🐂 Liberty Global Bull Case

data.title: Costamare (NYSE:CMRE) Margin Compression To 14.4% Puts Long Term Bull Case Under Scrutiny
data.description:
Costamare (NYSE:CMRE) has put up another solid set of shipping numbers, with third quarter FY 2025 revenue of US$225.2 million and EPS of US$0.77, while trailing twelve month revenue sits at about US$2.1 billion with EPS of US$2.50. Over the past few reported periods, revenue has ranged from US$217.9 million to US$548.4 million a quarter and EPS from US$0.25 to US$0.90. This gives investors a clearer view of how earnings power has shifted across the cycle and where current margins are settling. In that context, the latest figures keep the focus firmly on how sustainable Costamare’s margin profile is and what that may mean for shareholders from here.

See our full analysis for Costamare.

With the headline results on the table, the next step is to see how these numbers line up with the most widely held market narratives around Costamare’s profitability, risk profile, and long term earnings power.

See what the community is saying about Costamare

Advertisement

Margins Compress From 33.3% To 14.4%

  • Trailing net profit margin sits at 14.4% for the last 12 months, compared with 33.3% a year earlier, while trailing net income is US$299.96 million on US$2.1b of revenue.
  • Critics highlight that tighter margins could clash with the bullish view that long term charters and high fleet employment keep earnings steady, because:
    • Near 100% revenue days fixed for 2025 and an average charter duration of 3.2 years are meant to support stable cash flows. However, the margin compression to 14.4% shows costs or pricing still matter a lot.
    • The expectation that modern, fuel efficient ships and leasing income support margins is partly challenged by the step down from 33.3% to 14.4%. This points to pressure even with those advantages in place.
If you want to see how bullish investors connect these margin trends with charter contracts and future earnings, check out the detailed bull case for Costamare: 🐂 Costamare Bull Case

P/E Of 6.9x Versus US Market 19.4x

  • Costamare trades on a trailing P/E of 6.9x, below the US market P/E of 19.4x and the North American Shipping industry average of 7.7x, while still above the shipping peer group at 5.9x.
  • What is interesting for the bearish narrative is how this relatively low P/E sits against concerns about leverage and margins, because:
    • Bears point to high debt and an unstable dividend record. Those issues sit alongside a 14.4% trailing net margin that has stepped down from 33.3%, which they see as a sign that earnings quality could be tested if shipping conditions soften.
    • At the same time, a 6.9x P/E is not the very cheapest in the peer set, so investors who share the cautious view may argue that the current valuation still needs to reflect the combination of higher leverage and thinner margins.
Skeptics watching that 6.9x P/E and thinner margins may want the full cautious angle on Costamare: 🐻 Costamare Bear Case

DCF Fair Value US$9.95 Versus Price US$17.15

  • The provided DCF fair value in the dataset is US$9.95 a share, which sits well below the current share price of US$17.15 and the US$17.00 analyst price target reference, creating a clear gap between model value and market price.
  • Analysts' consensus view flags long term contracted revenue of US$2.5b and expected earnings of about US$285.2 million by 2028, and the tension with today’s valuation shows up in two places:
    • The stock’s 6.9x trailing P/E looks low versus the US market at 19.4x, which some investors may interpret as supportive. However, the DCF fair value of US$9.95 indicates that at least one cash flow model in the data is more cautious than the current US$17.15 price.
    • Consensus uses a 6.1x P/E on those 2028 earnings to back into its US$17.00 target and assumes use of a 12.3% discount rate, so readers need to decide whether those profit margin and revenue assumptions line up with the current 14.4% trailing margin and the earnings trend of US$299.96 million over the last 12 months.

data.title: Rogers (ROG) Turns Q4 Profit But TTM Losses Keep Bull Narratives Under Pressure
